records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Suffices for the Freed Person on the Eve of Arafah Regarding the Hajj of Islam
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2892 - It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "A servant was freed on the Day of Arafah."
Imam (as) said: "If he reaches one of the two stations (Arafah or Muzdalifah), then he has caught the Hajj."
records.arabicText: 2892 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَمْلُوكٌ أُعْتِقَ يَوْمَ عَرَفَةَ قَالَ «إِذَا أَدْرَكَ أَحَدَ اَلْمَوْقِفَيْنِ فَقَدْ أَدْرَكَ اَلْحَجَّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2892 - It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "A servant was freed on the Day of Arafah."
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he reaches one of the two stations (Arafah or Muzdalifah), then he has caught the Hajj."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Hajj of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2893 - Zurara narrated from one of the Imams (as), who said:
"If a man takes his young child for Hajj, he instructs him to recite the Talbiyah and obligates the Hajj upon him. If the child cannot recite the Talbiyah properly, the father recites it on his behalf. The child is carried for Tawaf, and prayers are performed on his behalf."
I asked: "What if they do not have the means to slaughter (an animal) on his behalf?"
Imam (as) said: "An animal is slaughtered for the children, while the adults fast. The same restrictions of Ihram regarding clothing and fragrance apply to the child. However, if the child kills game, the liability falls upon the father."
records.arabicText: 2893 - رَوَى زُرَارَةُ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا حَجَّ اَلرَّجُلُ بِابْنِهِ وَ هُوَ صَغِيرٌ، فَإِنَّهُ يَأْمُرُهُ أَنْ يُلَبِّيَ وَ يَفْرِضَ اَلْحَجَّ فَإِنْ لَمْ يُحْسِنْ أَنْ يُلَبِّيَ لَبَّى عَنْهُ وَ يُطَافُ بِهِ وَ يُصَلَّى عَنْهُ » قُلْتُ لَيْسَ لَهُمْ مَا يَذْبَحُونَ عَنْهُ قَالَ «يُذْبَحُ عَنِ اَلصِّغَارِ وَ يَصُومُ اَلْكِبَارُ وَ يُتَّقَى عَلَيْهِمْ مَا يُتَّقَى عَلَى اَلْمُحْرِمِ مِنَ اَلثِّيَابِ وَ اَلطِّيبِ فَإِنْ قَتَلَ صَيْداً فَعَلَى أَبِيهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2893 - Zurara narrated from one of the Imams (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If a man takes his young child for Hajj, he instructs him to recite the Talbiyah and obligates the Hajj upon him. If the child cannot recite the Talbiyah properly, the father recites it on his behalf. The child is carried for Tawaf, and prayers are performed on his behalf."
I asked: "What if they do not have the means to slaughter (an animal) on his behalf?"
Imam (as) said: "An animal is slaughtered for the children, while the adults fast. The same restrictions of Ihram regarding clothing and fragrance apply to the child. However, if the child kills game, the liability falls upon the father."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Hajj of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2894 - It was narrated from Ayyub, the brother of Udaym, who said:
Abu Abdullah (as) was asked: "From where are the children stripped (for Ihram)?"
Imam (as) said: "My father (as) used to strip them at Fakh."
records.arabicText: 2894 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَيُّوبَ أَخِي أُدَيْمٍ قَالَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مِنْ أَيْنَ يُجَرَّدُ اَلصِّبْيَانُ فَقَالَ «كَانَ أَبِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُجَرِّدُهُمْ مِنْ فَخٍّ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2894 - It was narrated from Ayyub, the brother of Udaym, who said:
Abu Abdullah (as) was asked: "From where are the children stripped (for Ihram)?"
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "My father (as) used to strip them at Fakh."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Hajj of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2895 - It was narrated from Yunus ibn Ya'qub, from his father, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I have young children with me, and I fear the cold for them. From where should they enter the state of Ihram?"
Imam (as) said: "Take them to Al-‘Arj, and let them enter Ihram from there, for when you reach Al-‘Arj, you will have entered Tihamah."
Then Imam (as) added: "If you still fear for them, take them to Al-Juhfah."
records.arabicText: 2895 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ يُونُسَ بْنِ يَعْقُوبَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِنَّ مَعِي صِبْيَةً صِغَاراً وَ أَنَا أَخَافُ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلْبَرْدَ فَمِنْ أَيْنَ يُحْرِمُونَ فَقَالَ «اِئْتِ بِهِمُ اَلْعَرْجَ فَلْيُحْرِمُوا مِنْهَا فَإِنَّكَ إِذَا أَتَيْتَ اَلْعَرْجَ وَقَعْتَ فِي تِهَامَةَ » ثُمَّ قَالَ «فَإِنْ خِفْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ فَائْتِ بِهِمُ اَلْجُحْفَةَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2895 - It was narrated from Yunus ibn Ya'qub, from his father, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I have young children with me, and I fear the cold for them. From where should they enter the state of Ihram?"
Imam (as) said: "Take them to Al-‘Arj, and let them enter Ihram from there, for when you reach Al-‘Arj, you will have entered Tihamah."
Then Imam (as) added: "If you still fear for them, take them to Al-Juhfah."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Hajj of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2896 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Look at the children who are with you. Take them to Al-Juhfah or Batn Marr, and treat them as you would treat those in the state of Ihram. Perform Tawaf for them, throw (the pebbles) on their behalf, and if they cannot offer the sacrifice, their guardian should fast on their behalf. Imam Ali ibn Al-Husayn (as) used to place the knife in the child's hand, then an adult would hold the child's hand to perform the slaughter."
records.arabicText: 2896 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اُنْظُرُوا مَنْ كَانَ مَعَكُمْ مِنَ اَلصِّبْيَانِ فَقَدِّمُوهُ إِلَى اَلْجُحْفَةِ أَوْ إِلَى بَطْنِ مَرٍّ وَ يُصْنَعُ بِهِمْ مَا يُصْنَعُ بِالْمُحْرِمِ وَ يُطَافُ بِهِمْ وَ يُرْمَى عَنْهُمْ وَ مَنْ لاَ يَجِدُ اَلْهَدْيَ مِنْهُمْ فَلْيَصُمْ عَنْهُ وَلِيُّهُ وَ كَانَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ يَضَعُ اَلسِّكِّينَ فِي يَدِ اَلصَّبِيِّ ثُمَّ يَقْبِضُ عَلَى يَدِهِ اَلرَّجُلُ فَيَذْبَحُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2896 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Look at the children who are with you. Take them to Al-Juhfah or Batn Marr, and treat them as you would treat those in the state of Ihram. Perform Tawaf for them, throw (the pebbles) on their behalf, and if they cannot offer the sacrifice, their guardian should fast on their behalf. Imam Ali ibn Al-Husayn (as) used to place the knife in the child's hand, then an adult would hold the child's hand to perform the slaughter."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Hajj of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2897 - It was narrated that Sama'ah asked Imam (as) about a man who instructed his servants to perform Tamattu‘ (Umrah and Hajj combined).
Imam (as) said: "It is incumbent upon him to offer the sacrifice on their behalf."
I said: "What if he gave them dirhams, and some of them offered the sacrifice while others kept the dirhams and chose to fast?"
Imam (as) said: "It is sufficient for them, and he has the choice to leave it as is if he wishes."
Imam (as) further said: "If he had commanded them to fast instead, it would have sufficed for them."
records.arabicText: 2897 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَمَاعَةُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ أَمَرَ غِلْمَانَهُ أَنْ يَتَمَتَّعُوا قَالَ «عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يُضَحِّيَ عَنْهُمْ» قُلْتُ فَإِنَّهُ أَعْطَاهُمْ دَرَاهِمَ فَبَعْضُهُمْ ضَحَّى وَ بَعْضُهُمْ أَمْسَكَ اَلدَّرَاهِمَ وَ صَامَ قَالَ «قَدْ أَجْزَأَ عَنْهُمْ وَ هُوَ بِالْخِيَارِ إِنْ شَاءَ تَرَكَهَا » قَالَ قَالَ «وَ لَوْ أَنَّهُ أَمَرَهُمْ فَصَامُوا كَانَ قَدْ أَجْزَأَ عَنْهُمْ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Hajj of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2898 - Safwan narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:
I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a ten-year-old boy performing Hajj.
Imam (as) said: "The Hajj of Islam becomes obligatory upon him if he reaches puberty, and likewise for a girl—it becomes obligatory upon her when she begins menstruating."
records.arabicText: 2898 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اِبْنِ عَشْرِ سِنِينَ يَحُجُّ قَالَ «عَلَيْهِ حَجَّةُ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ إِذَا اِحْتَلَمَ وَ كَذَلِكَ اَلْجَارِيَةُ عَلَيْهَا اَلْحَجُّ إِذَا طَمِثَتْ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2898 - Safwan narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar, who said:
I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a ten-year-old boy performing Hajj.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Hajj of Islam becomes obligatory upon him if he reaches puberty, and likewise for a girl—it becomes obligatory upon her when she begins menstruating."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Hajj of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2899 - It was narrated from Ali ibn Mahziyar, from Muhammad ibn al-Fudhayl, who said:
I asked Abu Ja'far al-Thani, Imam Muhammad al-Jawad (as) about when a child should be entered into the state of Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "When they begin to grow teeth."
records.arabicText: 2899 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مَهْزِيَارَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ اَلثَّانِيَ عَنِ اَلصَّبِيِّ مَتَى يُحْرَمُ بِهِ قَالَ «إِذَا اِثَّغَرَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2899 - It was narrated from Ali ibn Mahziyar, from Muhammad ibn al-Fudhayl, who said:
I asked Abu Ja'far al-Thani, Imam Muhammad al-Jawad (as) about when a child should be entered into the state of Ihram.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When they begin to grow teeth."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Hajj of Children
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2900 - Aban narrated from Al-Hakam, who said:
I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say: "If a child is taken for Hajj, it does not fulfill the Hajj of Islam when they grow up. Similarly, if a servant is taken for Hajj, it does not fulfill the Hajj of Islam until they are freed."
records.arabicText: 2900 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنِ اَلْحَكَمِ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ : «اَلصَّبِيُّ إِذَا حُجَّ بِهِ فَقَدْ قَضَى حَجَّةَ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ حَتَّى يَكْبَرَ وَ اَلْعَبْدُ إِذَا حُجَّ بِهِ فَقَدْ قَضَى حَجَّةَ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ حَتَّى يُعْتَقَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2900 - Aban narrated from Al-Hakam, who said:
I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a child is taken for Hajj, it does not fulfill the Hajj of Islam when they grow up. Similarly, if a servant is taken for Hajj, it does not fulfill the Hajj of Islam until they are freed."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Borrowing to Perform Hajj and the Obligation of Hajj on Someone in Debt
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2901 - It was narrated from Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who performs Hajj with borrowed money and has already performed the Hajj of Islam.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, and Allah (swt), the Exalted, will settle his debt, God willing."
records.arabicText: 2901 - رُوِيَ عَنْ يَعْقُوبَ بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ يَحُجُّ بِدَيْنٍ وَ قَدْ حَجَّ حَجَّةَ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ إِنَّ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ سَيَقْضِي عَنْهُ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2901 - It was narrated from Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who performs Hajj with borrowed money and has already performed the Hajj of Islam.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Yes, and Allah (swt), the Exalted, will settle his debt, God willing."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Borrowing to Perform Hajj and the Obligation of Hajj on Someone in Debt
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2902 - It was narrated from Abdul-Malik ibn Utbah, who said:
I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who has debt but borrows money to perform Hajj.
Imam (as) said: "If he has a means to repay the debt, there is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2902 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ يَسْتَقْرِضُ وَ يَحُجُّ قَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ وَجْهٌ فِي مَالٍ فَلاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2902 - It was narrated from Abdul-Malik ibn Utbah, who said:
I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who has debt but borrows money to perform Hajj.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If he has a means to repay the debt, there is no harm in it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Borrowing to Perform Hajj and the Obligation of Hajj on Someone in Debt
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2903 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Imam (as), who said:
I asked him: "Can a man borrow money to perform Hajj if he has resources left behind to settle the debt in case something happens to him?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 2903 - وَ رَوَى مُوسَى بْنُ بَكْرٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لَهُ هَلْ يَسْتَقْرِضُ اَلرَّجُلُ وَ يَحُجُّ إِذَا كَانَ خَلْفَ ظَهْرِهِ مَا يُؤَدَّى بِهِ عَنْهُ إِذَا حَدَثَ بِهِ حَدَثٌ قَالَ «نَعَمْ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2903 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Imam (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked him: "Can a man borrow money to perform Hajj if he has resources left behind to settle the debt in case something happens to him?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Borrowing to Perform Hajj and the Obligation of Hajj on Someone in Debt
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2904 - It was narrated from Abu Hammam, who said:
I said to Al-Ridha (as): "A man has debt, and some wealth comes to him. Should he pay his debt or perform Hajj?"
Imam (as) said: "He should pay part of it toward his debt and use part for Hajj."
I said: "What if the amount is only sufficient for the expenses of Hajj?"
Imam (as) said: "Let him pay his debt one year and perform Hajj the next year."
I asked: "What if the money comes from the government?"
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm for you in that."
records.arabicText: 2904 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي هَمَّامٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ اَلرَّجُلُ يَكُونُ عَلَيْهِ اَلدَّيْنُ وَ يَحْضُرُهُ اَلشَّيْءُ أَ يَقْضِي دَيْنَهُ أَوْ يَحُجُّ قَالَ «يَقْضِي بِبَعْضٍ وَ يَحُجُّ بِبَعْضٍ» قُلْتُ فَإِنَّهُ لاَ يَكُونُ إِلاَّ بِقَدْرِ نَفَقَةِ اَلْحَجِّ قَالَ «يَقْضِي سَنَةً وَ يَحُجُّ سَنَةً» قُلْتُ أُعْطِيَ اَلْمَالَ مِنْ نَاحِيَةِ اَلسُّلْطَانِ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2904 - It was narrated from Abu Hammam, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Al-Ridha (as): "A man has debt, and some wealth comes to him. Should he pay his debt or perform Hajj?"
Imam (as) said: "He should pay part of it toward his debt and use part for Hajj."
I said: "What if the amount is only sufficient for the expenses of Hajj?"
Imam (as) said: "Let him pay his debt one year and perform Hajj the next year."
I asked: "What if the money comes from the government?"
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm for you in that."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Borrowing to Perform Hajj and the Obligation of Hajj on Someone in Debt
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2905 - A man asked Abu Abdullah (as): "I am a man in debt. Should I borrow money and perform Hajj?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, it is more effective in settling the debt."
records.arabicText: 2905 - وَ سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فَقَالَ لَهُ إِنِّي رَجُلٌ ذُو دَيْنٍ فَأَتَدَيَّنُ وَ أَحُجُّ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ هُوَ أَقْضَى لِلدَّيْنِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2905 - A man asked Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I am a man in debt. Should I borrow money and perform Hajj?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, it is more effective in settling the debt."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Borrowing to Perform Hajj and the Obligation of Hajj on Someone in Debt
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2906 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Aban, from Al-Hasan ibn Ziyad al-‘Attar, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I have debt, and some dirhams come into my possession. If I divide them among my creditors, they will not settle anything significant. Should I use them for Hajj, or should I distribute them among the creditors?"
Imam (as) said: "Perform Hajj with them, and pray to Allah (swt) to help you settle your debt, God willing."
records.arabicText: 2906 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبَانٍ عَنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ اَلْعَطَّارِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَكُونُ عَلَيَّ اَلدَّيْنُ فَيَقَعُ فِي يَدِيَ اَلدَّرَاهِمُ فَإِنْ وَزَّعْتُهَا بَيْنَهُمْ لَمْ يَقَعْ شَيْئاً أَ فَأَحُجُّ أَوْ أُوَزِّعُهَا بَيْنَ اَلْغُرَمَاءِ قَالَ «حُجَّ بِهَا وَ اُدْعُ اَللَّهَ أَنْ يَقْضِيَ عَنْكَ دَيْنَكَ إِنْ شَاءَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2906 - Ibn Mahbub narrated from Aban, from Al-Hasan ibn Ziyad al-‘Attar, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "I have debt, and some dirhams come into my possession. If I divide them among my creditors, they will not settle anything significant. Should I use them for Hajj, or should I distribute them among the creditors?"
Imam (as) said: "Perform Hajj with them, and pray to Allah (swt) to help you settle your debt, God willing."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding a Woman Whose Husband Prevents Her From Performing the Obligatory Hajj or a Voluntary Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2907 - Aban narrated from Zurara, from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
I asked him about a woman who has a husband, has not performed Hajj before (ṣarūra), and her husband does not permit her to go for Hajj.
Imam (as) said: "She should perform Hajj, even if he does not permit her."
records.arabicText: 2907 - رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اِمْرَأَةٍ لَهَا زَوْجٌ وَ هِيَ صَرُورَةٌ وَ لاَ يَأْذَنُ لَهَا فِي اَلْحَجِّ قَالَ «تَحُجُّ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَأْذَنْ لَهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2907 - Aban narrated from Zurara, from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
I asked him about a woman who has a husband, has not performed Hajj before (ṣarūra), and her husband does not permit her to go for Hajj.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "She should perform Hajj, even if he does not permit her."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding a Woman Whose Husband Prevents Her From Performing the Obligatory Hajj or a Voluntary Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2908 - In a narration from Abdul-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as),
Imam (as) said: "She should perform Hajj, even if it displeases him."
records.arabicText: 2908 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «تَحُجُّ وَ إِنْ رَغِمَ أَنْفُهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2908 - In a narration from Abdul-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah, from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as),
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "She should perform Hajj, even if it displeases him."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding a Woman Whose Husband Prevents Her From Performing the Obligatory Hajj or a Voluntary Hajj
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2909 - Ishaq ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Ibrahim (as), who said:
I asked him about a wealthy woman who had already performed the Hajj of Islam but said to her husband, "Take me for Hajj again." Can he prevent her from going?
Imam (as) said: "Yes, he can say to her, 'My right over you is greater than your right over me in this matter.'"
records.arabicText: 2909 - وَ رَوَى إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ اَلْمُوسِرَةِ قَدْ حَجَّتْ حَجَّةَ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ فَتَقُولُ لِزَوْجِهَا أَ حِجَّنِي مَرَّةً أُخْرَى أَ لَهُ أَنْ يَمْنَعَهَا قَالَ «نَعَمْ يَقُولُ لَهَا حَقِّي عَلَيْكِ أَعْظَمُ مِنْ حَقِّكِ عَلَيَّ فِي ذَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2909 - Ishaq ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Ibrahim (as), who said:
I asked him about a wealthy woman who had already performed the Hajj of Islam but said to her husband,
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Take me for Hajj again." Can he prevent her from going?
Imam (as) said: "Yes, he can say to her, 'My right over you is greater than your right over me in this matter.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Woman Performing Hajj Without a Mahram or Guardian
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2910 - It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a woman traveling to Mecca without a guardian.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm if she travels with trustworthy people."
records.arabicText: 2910 - رُوِيَ عَنْ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ تَخْرُجُ إِلَى مَكَّةَ بِغَيْرِ وَلِيٍّ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ تَخْرُجُ مَعَ قَوْمٍ ثِقَاتٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2910 - It was narrated from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a woman traveling to Mecca without a guardian.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm if she travels with trustworthy people."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Woman Performing Hajj Without a Mahram or Guardian
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2911 - In a narration from Hisham, from Sulayman ibn Khalid, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a woman who wants to perform Hajj but does not have a mahram (close male relative), he (as) was asked: "Is it permissible for her to perform Hajj?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if she is trustworthy."
records.arabicText: 2911 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ هِشَامٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلْمَرْأَةِ تُرِيدُ اَلْحَجَّ وَ لَيْسَ مَعَهَا مَحْرَمٌ هَلْ يَصْلُحُ لَهَا اَلْحَجُّ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ إِذَا كَانَتْ مَأْمُونَةً ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2911 - In a narration from Hisham, from Sulayman ibn Khalid, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a woman who wants to perform Hajj but does not have a mahram (close male relative), he (as) was asked:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Is it permissible for her to perform Hajj?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if she is trustworthy."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Woman Performing Hajj Without a Mahram or Guardian
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2912 - Al-Bazanti narrated from Safwan al-Jammal, who said:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "You know my profession. A woman comes to me, and I know her to be Muslim, to love you, and to hold your guardianship (wilayah), but she has no mahram (close male relative)."
Imam (as) said: "If a Muslim woman comes, then take her, for a believer is a mahram for a believing woman." He then recited the verse: 'And the believing men and believing women are allies of one another.' (Surah At-Tawbah 9:71).
records.arabicText: 2912 - وَ رَوَى اَلْبَزَنْطِيُّ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ اَلْجَمَّالِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَدْ عَرَفْتَنِي بِعَمَلِي تَأْتِينِي اَلْمَرْأَةُ أَعْرِفُهَا بِإِسْلاَمِهَا وَ حُبِّهَا إِيَّاكُمْ وَ وَلاَيَتِهَا لَكُمْ لَيْسَ لَهَا مَحْرَمٌ قَالَ «إِذَا جَاءَتِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ اَلْمُسْلِمَةُ فَاحْمِلْهَا فَإِنَّ اَلْمُؤْمِنَ مَحْرَمُ اَلْمُؤْمِنَةِ » ثُمَّ تَلاَ هَذِهِ اَلْآيَةَ ۝ وَ اَلْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَ اَلْمُؤْمِنٰاتُ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيٰاءُ بَعْضٍ۝
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2912 - Al-Bazanti narrated from Safwan al-Jammal, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I said to Abu Abdullah (as): "You know my profession. A woman comes to me, and I know her to be Muslim, to love you, and to hold your guardianship (wilayah), but she has no mahram (close male relative)."
Imam (as) said: "If a Muslim woman comes, then take her, for a believer is a mahram for a believing woman." He then recited the verse: 'And the believing men and believing women are allies of one another.' (Surah At-Tawbah 9:71).

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Woman Performing Hajj During the Iddah (waiting period)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2913 - Al-'Ala' narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (as), who said:
"A divorced woman may perform Hajj during her waiting period ('iddah)."
records.arabicText: 2913 - رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْمُطَلَّقَةُ تَحُجُّ فِي عِدَّتِهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2913 - Al-'Ala' narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A divorced woman may perform Hajj during her waiting period ('iddah)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Woman Performing Hajj During the Iddah (waiting period)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2914 - Ibn Bukayr narrated from Zurara, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a woman whose husband has passed away: "Can she perform Hajj during her waiting period ('iddah)?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 2914 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ بُكَيْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ اَلَّتِي يُتَوَفَّى عَنْهَا زَوْجُهَا أَ تَحُجُّ فِي عِدَّتِهَا قَالَ «نَعَمْ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2914 - Ibn Bukayr narrated from Zurara, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a woman whose husband has passed away:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Can she perform Hajj during her waiting period ('iddah)?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Pilgrim Who Dies on the Way
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2915 - Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Durays, from Abu Ja'far (as), regarding a man who set out to perform the Hajj of Islam but died on the way.
Imam (as) said: "If he died within the sacred precinct (haram), it suffices as the Hajj of Islam. But if he died outside the haram, his heir should perform the Hajj of Islam on his behalf."
records.arabicText: 2915 - رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ رِئَابٍ عَنْ ضُرَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ خَرَجَ حَاجّاً حَجَّةَ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ فَمَاتَ فِي اَلطَّرِيقِ فَقَالَ «إِنْ مَاتَ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ فَقَدْ أَجْزَأَتْ عَنْهُ حَجَّةُ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ مَاتَ دُونَ اَلْحَرَمِ فَلْيَقْضِ عَنْهُ وَلِيُّهُ حَجَّةَ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2915 - Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Durays, from Abu Ja'far (as), regarding a man who set out to perform the Hajj of Islam but died on the way.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If he died within the sacred precinct (haram), it suffices as the Hajj of Islam. But if he died outside the haram, his heir should perform the Hajj of Islam on his behalf."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Pilgrim Who Dies on the Way
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2916 - Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Burayd al-‘Ijli, who said:
I asked Abu Ja'far (as) about a man who set out for Hajj with his camel, provisions, and expenses, but died on the way.
Imam (as) said: "If he was a ṣarūra (someone who had not performed the Hajj of Islam) and died within the haram, it suffices as the Hajj of Islam. If he died as a ṣarūra before entering into Ihram, his camel, provisions, expenses, and all that he had with him should be used to fulfill the Hajj of Islam on his behalf. If there is anything left over, it goes to his heirs, provided he had no debts."
I asked: "What if the Hajj was voluntary, and he died on the way before entering into Ihram? What happens to his camel, expenses, and belongings?"
Imam (as) said: "Everything he had and left behind goes to his heirs unless he had debts, in which case they are paid from his estate. If he left a will, it is carried out for the person he designated, with the will being fulfilled from one-third of his estate."
records.arabicText: 2916 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ رِئَابٍ عَنْ بُرَيْدٍ اَلْعِجْلِيِّ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ خَرَجَ حَاجّاً وَ مَعَهُ جَمَلٌ لَهُ وَ نَفَقَةٌ وَ زَادٌ فَمَاتَ فِي اَلطَّرِيقِ قَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ صَرُورَةً ثُمَّ مَاتَ فِي اَلْحَرَمِ فَقَدْ أَجْزَأَتْ عَنْهُ حَجَّةُ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ مَاتَ وَ هُوَ صَرُورَةٌ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ جُعِلَ جَمَلُهُ وَ زَادُهُ وَ نَفَقَتُهُ وَ مَا مَعَهُ فِي حَجَّةِ اَلْإِسْلاَمِ، فَإِنْ فَضَلَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ شَيْءٌ فَهُوَ لِلْوَرَثَةِ إِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ» قُلْتُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ كَانَتِ اَلْحَجَّةُ تَطَوُّعاً ثُمَّ مَاتَ فِي اَلطَّرِيقِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يُحْرِمَ لِمَنْ يَكُونُ جَمَلُهُ وَ نَفَقَتُهُ وَ مَا مَعَهُ قَالَ «يَكُونُ جَمِيعُ مَا مَعَهُ وَ مَا تَرَكَ لِلْوَرَثَةِ إِلاَّ أَنْ يَكُونَ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ فَيُقْضَى عَنْهُ أَوْ يَكُونَ أَوْصَى بِوَصِيَّةٍ فَيَنْفُذَ ذَلِكَ لِمَنْ أَوْصَى لَهُ وَ يُجْعَلَ ذَلِكَ مِنْ ثُلُثِهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2916 - Ali ibn Ri'ab narrated from Burayd al-‘Ijli, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Abu Ja'far (as) about a man who set out for Hajj with his camel, provisions, and expenses, but died on the way.
Imam (as) said: "If he was a ṣarūra (someone who had not performed the Hajj of Islam) and died within the haram, it suffices as the Hajj of Islam. If he died as a ṣarūra before entering into Ihram, his camel, provisions, expenses, and all that he had with him should be used to fulfill the Hajj of Islam on his behalf. If there is anything left over, it goes to his heirs, provided he had no debts."
I asked: "What if the Hajj was voluntary, and he died on the way before entering into Ihram? What happens to his camel, expenses, and belongings?"
Imam (as) said: "Everything he had and left behind goes to his heirs unless he had debts, in which case they are paid from his estate. If he left a will, it is carried out for the person he designated, with the will being fulfilled from one-third of his estate."
